applicabilityConditions: Customer demand and capacity urgency remain observable and infrastructure economics remain supportable.
recommendedAction: Scale infrastructure while constraining operating expense growth and monitoring infrastructure economics.
nonApplicabilityConditions: Project-specific demand, adoption, timing, location, or infrastructure economics no longer supports the original commitment.
guardrails: Keep operating-expense growth and disciplined profitability explicit while capital capacity scales.
Adjust reversible early-stage assets before abandoning the broader AI infrastructure strategy.
exceptions: Slow or pause selected early-stage projects when updated demand, adoption, or business-priority signals weaken the original timing or location.
Use staged or third-party capacity when it preserves flexibility without weakening service commitments.
reversalSignals: Customer demand weakens, service adoption trails plan, capacity urgency relaxes, project location becomes suboptimal, or infrastructure economics materially deteriorates.
